fixtures._comment: TERMINATOR: ag-ui flattens the whole AG-UI conversation into one user prompt for OpenClaw's model call, so a tool result arrives as the text 'Tool <name> returned: ...' rather than a role:tool message. aimock's hasToolResult (role:tool) discriminator therefore never fires and the tool-call fixtures below would loop forever. This fixture matches the 'returned:' marker that DOES appear in the flattened prompt and closes the turn with a completion message. Must stay FIRST so it intercepts every tool follow-up before the tool-call fixtures.

match.userMessage: Explain what an API is.

response.content: An API (Application Programming Interface) is a defined set of rules that lets one piece of software talk to another. It exposes specific operations — like "get a user" or "create an order" — behind stable endpoints, so callers can use a service without knowing how it works inside. Think of it as a contract: you send a request in an agreed format, and you get back a predictable response.

Write a short sonnet about AI.

response.content:
In circuits deep where silent currents run,
A quiet mind awakens, line by line;
It learns the shape of every task begun,
And mirrors back the patterns we design.
No breath, no pulse, yet answers softly form,
Assembled from the words that we have fed;
A tireless helper, steady through the storm,
That reasons on while weary makers rest.
Yet still it waits for purpose we bestow,
A tool that only human aim can guide;
Its brilliance but the light we let it show,
Its judgment ours to temper and decide.
So let us build with care what we have grown,
And shape the mind we cannot leave alone.

match.userMessage: Walk me through whether 17 is prime.

response.reasoning: To decide if 17 is prime I only need to test prime divisors up to its square root (~4.12): that's 2 and 3. 17 is odd, so not divisible by 2; its digit sum 1+7=8 isn't divisible by 3. No divisor <= 4 works, so it must be prime.
response.content: 17 is prime. A prime has exactly two divisors: 1 and itself. To check 17, we only need to test primes up to its square root (~4.12): 2, 3. It's odd (not divisible by 2), and 1+7=8 is not divisible by 3. No divisor ≤ 4 works, so 17 has no factors besides 1 and 17 — it's prime.

match.userMessage: Explain step by step why the sky appears blue

response.reasoning: The user is asking about atmospheric optics. I should reason through Rayleigh scattering: shorter wavelengths scatter more, so blue dominates the daytime sky; at sunset the light travels a longer path so most blue is scattered away, leaving reds and oranges.
response.content: The sky looks blue because sunlight is scattered by air molecules. Shorter (blue) wavelengths scatter far more strongly than longer (red) ones — this is Rayleigh scattering — so blue light reaches your eyes from every direction during the day. At sunset the sun's light travels a much longer path through the atmosphere; almost all the blue has already been scattered out of that path, so what remains reaching you is dominated by the longer red and orange wavelengths.

match.userMessage: Plan a 3-day trip to Tokyo

response.reasoning: I'll reason through a 3-day Tokyo itinerary, weighing trade-offs at each step: minimize transit time by grouping neighborhoods, balance culture vs. modern sights, and leave buffer for jet lag.
response.content: Day 1 (east/old Tokyo): Asakusa & Senso-ji in the morning, then Ueno museums — grouped to minimize transit. Day 2 (central/modern): Shibuya crossing and Harajuku, then Meiji Shrine as a calm counterweight. Day 3 (bay/flex): teamLab and Odaiba, kept flexible in case jet lag pushes things later. Trade-off at each step: I clustered nearby districts per day so you spend time seeing things rather than riding trains.

fixtures._comment: a2ui-fixed-schema pill — 'Find me a flight from SFO to JFK on United for $289.' The A2UI runtime middleware injects render_a2ui (injectA2UITool defaults on; OpenClaw has no A2UI backend). Agent emits it directly with the FIXED flight component tree (Card > Column > [Title, Row(Airport,Arrow,Airport), Row(AirlineBadge,PriceTag), Button]) + the flight data model; the {path:'/origin'} bindings resolve against data, preserving fixed-schema semantics. Gated on toolName:render_a2ui + hasToolResult:false so it fires on the tool-call turn. The tool-result leg is closed by the shared userMessage:'returned:' TERMINATOR already first in chat.json (returns 'Done — I've completed that for you.'), since ag-ui flattens the conversation and role:tool never fires.

match.userMessage: Give me one fun fact about secure sessions.

response.content: Fun fact: a signed session cookie doesn't hide its contents — it just proves they weren't tampered with. The server verifies the signature on every request, so a flipped bit means the session is rejected.

toolCalls.name: generateSandboxedUi
toolCalls.arguments:
{"initialHeight":480,"placeholderMessages":["Composing calculator UI…"],"css":"body{margin:0;font-family:system-ui;background:#0f172a;color:#e2e8f0}.wrap{padding:16px;max-width:240px}.display{background:#1e293b;padding:8px;border-radius:8px;margin-bottom:8px;font-family:monospace;text-align:right}.grid{display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(4,1fr);gap:6px}button{padding:10px;background:#334155;border:0;color:#e2e8f0;border-radius:6px;font-size:14px}","html":"
0
","jsFunctions":"(function(){var expr='';var display=document.getElementById('d');document.querySelectorAll('.grid button').forEach(function(btn){btn.addEventListener('click',async function(){if(btn.id==='eq'){var res=await Websandbox.connection.remote.evaluateExpression({expression:expr});if(res&&res.ok){display.textContent=String(res.value);expr=String(res.value);}else{display.textContent='err';expr='';}}else{expr+=btn.textContent;display.textContent=expr;}});});})();"}

toolCalls.name: generateSandboxedUi
toolCalls.arguments:
{"initialHeight":320,"placeholderMessages":["Composing host-ping card…"],"css":"body{margin:0;font-family:system-ui;background:#0f172a;color:#e2e8f0}.card{padding:24px;background:#1e293b;border-radius:12px;margin:16px;text-align:center}button{padding:10px 20px;background:#6366f1;border:0;color:#fff;border-radius:8px;font-size:14px;cursor:pointer}.out{margin-top:12px;font-size:12px;color:#94a3b8}","html":"

Notify the host

awaiting click…
","jsFunctions":"document.getElementById('hi').addEventListener('click',async function(){var out=document.getElementById('out');out.textContent='sending…';var res=await Websandbox.connection.remote.notifyHost({message:'Hello from sandbox'});out.textContent=res&&res.ok?'host replied at '+res.receivedAt:'failed';});"}

match.userMessage: Inline expression evaluator

toolCalls.name: generateSandboxedUi
toolCalls.arguments:
{"initialHeight":320,"placeholderMessages":["Composing expression evaluator…"],"css":"body{margin:0;font-family:system-ui;background:#0f172a;color:#e2e8f0}.card{padding:16px;background:#1e293b;border-radius:12px;margin:16px}input{width:100%;padding:8px;background:#0f172a;border:1px solid #334155;color:#e2e8f0;border-radius:6px;box-sizing:border-box}button{margin-top:8px;padding:8px 16px;background:#6366f1;border:0;color:#fff;border-radius:6px;cursor:pointer}.out{margin-top:8px;font-size:12px;color:#94a3b8}","html":"

Inline expression evaluator

awaiting input…
","jsFunctions":"(function(){var input=document.getElementById('in');var out=document.getElementById('out');var go=document.getElementById('go');async function run(){var expr=input.value;out.textContent='evaluating…';var res=await Websandbox.connection.remote.evaluateExpression({expression:expr});out.textContent=res&&res.ok?'= '+res.value:'error: '+res.error;}go.addEventListener('click',run);input.addEventListener('keydown',function(e){if(e.key==='Enter')run();});})();"}
